title: Dequeue Next Highest-Priority Patient
description: The system shall dequeue the patient with the highest priority from the queue and present their details to the physician.
rationale: This requirement ensures that patients receive timely care based on their urgency, improving overall patient satisfaction and reducing wait times for critical cases.
business_value: By prioritizing patient care, this feature enhances hospital efficiency, reduces operational costs associated with prolonged patient stays, and improves patient outcomes.

source.description: As a physician I need to dequeue the next highest-priority patient from the queue so that I can attend to them

acceptance_criteria: the system returns the patient with the highest priority
that patient is removed from the queue
preconditions: there is a queue of patients with different priorities
postconditions: the physician has been presented with the details of the next highest-priority patient
the patient is no longer in the queue
functional_constraints: The patient queue must be sorted by priority.
Dequeuing removes the patient from the queue and presents their details to the physician.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system should respond within 5 seconds for a dequeue request under normal conditions.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle up to 100 concurrent physician requests without degradation in performance.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling should provide clear and concise messages to the physician.

edge_cases: What happens if the patient queue is empty when the physician attempts to dequeue a patient?
How should the system handle situations where multiple patients have the same highest priority?
What if there is a network failure during the dequeue process?
What happens if the system receives concurrent dequeue requests from different physicians?

interfaces.name: Patient Queue Dequeue API
interfaces.description: Provides an endpoint for physicians to request the next highest-priority patient from the queue.

interfaces.name: Patient Queue Database
interfaces.description: Stores and manages the list of patients with their respective priorities. The system interacts with this database to retrieve and update patient information during the dequeue process.

interfaces.name: Physician Interface
interfaces.description: The physician interface allows physicians to initiate a dequeue request. It displays the details of the dequeued patient once the request is processed.

test_cases.description: Dequeue patient from an empty queue.
test_cases.expected_result: System should return an error message indicating that the queue is empty.

test_cases.description: Dequeue patient with highest priority when multiple patients have same priority.
test_cases.expected_result: System should dequeue a patient based on their secondary priority (if configured) and present their details to the physician.
System should handle network failures gracefully, log the error, and attempt reconnection or notify maintenance team.

test_cases.description: Simultaneous dequeue requests from different physicians.
test_cases.expected_result: System should manage concurrent requests efficiently, ensuring that each physician receives their highest-priority patient without conflict.
